draftEmail.body:
Hi Business Owner,

I was looking at 3 Men Movers - Dallas online and noticed your excellent 4.8-star rating from nearly 1000 reviews – that's a seriously strong reputation you've built!

However, I also noticed a few small technical details on your Dallas service page that might be costing you some easy wins online.

Specifically, I saw that 10 of your images aren't loading as efficiently as they could, and there are about 400 bits of 'inline' code that are making your site work harder than it needs to. Plus, 23 images are missing descriptions, which hurts how they show up in searches.

What's interesting is that your competitor, Wildcat Movers - Dallas, has already addressed these exact issues on their site. While your overall online score is a solid 88 compared to their 82, these specific areas are places where they're currently outperforming you.

These aren't huge fixes, but they can make a real difference in how quickly your site loads for potential customers and how easily people find you through search engines.

draftEmail.subject: Evolution Moving: Quick thought on speed
draftEmail.body:
Hi Ryan and Cameron,

Karm Joshi here from FourIqTech. I was poking around your website, evolutionmoving.com, and I was really impressed by your 4.9/5.0 rating from over 3,100 customers – that's fantastic and clearly shows you're doing a great job for your clients.

However, my team ran a quick technical check, and we noticed something that might be holding back potential new customers from seeing that amazing service.

While your site scores well overall at 86/100, we found your 'speed' score is sitting at 50/100. Digging deeper, it looks like you've got 156 images that aren't set up to 'lazy load' (meaning they all try to load at once), and there are 18 JavaScript and 7 CSS files that could be combined for better performance.

What's interesting is that even though Wildcat Movers - Dallas has a slightly lower overall score at 82/100, they've already tackled these specific speed issues. This means they might be offering a smoother, faster experience to visitors who land on their site, which can make a big difference when someone is looking for a mover.

A slower site often means people just click away, especially on their phones, before they even get a chance to see your great reviews or request a quote.

I've put together a super quick 10-minute overview of what's happening and how simple fixes could help you capture more of those potential customers. Would you be open to a brief chat sometime this week? No pressure at all, just sharing some insights.

Best,
Karm Joshi
FourIqTech

draftEmail.subject: Your Website vs. Plumbing NYC
draftEmail.body:
Hi Business Owner,

I was looking at A&E NYC Plumbing's website, topplumbernyc.com, and noticed you've got a pretty solid online presence with an overall score of 76. That's a good foundation.

However, I also saw that one of your main competitors, Plumbing NYC (plumbingnyc.com), is currently scoring 83. They're ahead of you in a few key areas that directly impact how many calls you get.

For example, your website's speed is a notable gap. You're at 50/100, while Plumbing NYC is at 65/100. This isn't just a number; it means potential customers might leave your site sooner if it takes even a second too long to load. Our audit showed you have 21 JavaScript and 31 CSS files, which definitely slow things down. They've also got a better website structure score (90 vs your 80) partly because they don't have a missing H1 heading like your site does, which hurts how Google understands what your page is about.

I've done a deeper dive and found a few specific, easy-to-fix issues that are holding you back from catching up and even surpassing them. I'd love to show you exactly what these are and how fixing them could bring in more leads for your business.

Would you be open to a quick 10 or 15-minute chat next week? No pressure, just a straightforward look at the data.
